messages.content: What's the Effect v3 way to log a value passing through a pipeline without changing its type? Show a minimal example.
Add structured logging to this Effect v3 pipeline without breaking the typed success value: `const placeOrder = (o: Order) => chargeCard(o).pipe(Effect.flatMap(persistOrder))`.
Show me the Effect v3 equivalent of adding a `console.log` inside a `.then(...)` without changing the promise value. Brief example.
What's the Effect v3 way to log ONLY when an effect fails, without swallowing the error? Minimal example.
Explain briefly how `Effect.tapBoth` differs from `Effect.tap` + `Effect.tapError`, then show a working typed implementation.
Write an Effect v3 wrapper that adds metrics around any effect. Constraints: use Effect.tap for success count, Effect.tapError for failure count, strict types, no any.
Add a latency-metric tap to this Effect v3 call: `const queryDb = (sql: string) => runSql(sql)`.
Refactor this Effect v3 code to use `Effect.tap` instead of `Effect.flatMap` for the logging step: `fetchUser(id).pipe(Effect.flatMap((u) => Effect.as(Console.log(u.name), u)))`.
Implement an Effect v3 cache-miss tracer with strict types. The effect should lookup from cache, tap the miss branch with a log, then fall back to the source.
Add an audit-trail tap to this Effect v3 update: `const updateOrder = (o: Order, patch: Patch) => applyPatch(o, patch)`.
What's the Effect v3 way to tap the error of a retry so each attempt is logged? Show a minimal typed example.
Build an Effect v3 helper `traced<A,E,R>(label, eff)` that emits start/end logs using Effect.tapBoth.
Fix this Effect v3 fragment — `Effect.flatMap((u) => Console.log(u)).pipe(Effect.map(() => u))` — it no longer compiles because `u` is out of scope. Use Effect.tap.
Refactor this Effect v3 pipeline to co-locate OK and ERROR instrumentation using Effect.tapBoth: `sendEmail(msg).pipe(Effect.tap((_) => incSent()), Effect.tapError((_) => incFailed()))`.
Implement a request-context logger in Effect v3 that stamps every log line with a requestId via Effect.tap. Strict types, no any.
Build an Effect v3 HTTP middleware pipeline using Effect.gen — parse body, authenticate, authorize, handle, serialize response. Strict types, tagged errors.
Write an Effect v3 database-transaction runner using Effect.gen: begin, run body, commit, rollback on failure. Strict types, tagged errors.
Build an Effect v3 auth-token refresh effect using Effect.gen — read cached token, if expired call refresh, store new token, return the valid one.
Implement an event-bus subscriber loop in Effect v3 using Effect.gen — take from queue, decode, dispatch, ack, repeat. Strict types.
Write an Effect v3 circuit breaker using Effect.gen — track consecutive failures in a Ref, open the circuit after N, fail fast while open.
Build an Effect v3 caching layer using Effect.gen — check cache, on miss call source, populate cache, return value. Strict types.
Write an Effect-based Express-style handler in Effect v3 using Effect.gen that reads params, loads a resource, and returns a typed response object. Strict types.
Implement an Effect v3 rate-limited API caller using Effect.gen — a semaphore bounds concurrency, then the wrapped effect runs normally. Strict types.
Build an Effect v3 pagination crawler using Effect.gen — loop fetching pages until `nextCursor` is null, accumulate results.
Write an Effect v3 health-check aggregator using Effect.gen — run multiple probes, tally ok/failed, fail overall if any critical probe fails.
Implement an Effect v3 background job runner using Effect.gen — dequeue jobs, process with a handler, record success/failure metrics.
Write an Effect v3 webhook receiver using Effect.gen — verify signature, parse payload, look up subscription, dispatch. Strict types, tagged errors.
Build an Effect v3 dead-letter handler using Effect.gen — run an effect, on failure publish to DLQ, otherwise return the value.
Implement an Effect v3 file-upload pipeline using Effect.gen — validate MIME type, compute sha256, upload to blob store, persist metadata. Strict types, tagged errors.
Write an Effect v3 distributed lock acquire-run-release helper using Effect.gen. Strict types, tagged errors.
Build an Effect v3 batched DB writer using Effect.gen — accumulate N records, flush as one SQL insert, repeat.
Write an Effect v3 graceful-shutdown coordinator using Effect.gen — fire shutdown handlers in registration order, swallow individual failures, log each.
Build an Effect v3 outbox-pattern publisher using Effect.gen — read pending rows, publish to topic, mark sent, commit.
Implement an Effect v3 saga coordinator using Effect.gen — run steps in order, on failure run compensations in reverse. Strict types.
Build an Effect v3 command dispatcher using Effect.gen — decode command type, look up handler, execute, tagged-error fallthrough.
Implement an Effect v3 inbound request validator using Effect.gen — check schema, enforce quota, persist, emit audit event. Strict types, tagged errors.
Write an Effect v3 fan-out broadcast using Effect.gen — for each subscriber, call deliver, collect failures in an array, succeed overall if at least one delivered.
Build an Effect v3 SSE (server-sent events) stream builder using Effect.gen — subscribe to a source, format each event, send to writer, loop.
Implement an Effect v3 token-bucket rate limiter using Effect.gen — refill at a rate, block until a permit is available, then run.
Explain briefly how Effect.tap fits into logging-heavy pipelines without changing the return type, then show a working typed implementation with three taps.
